title: Molten Roasted Russian Vegetables Roast
description: A hearty, comforting Russian-inspired roasted vegetable dish with a spicy kick from Madras paste. Perfect for a solo dinner on a cold evening.
instructions: Peel and chop the carrots and potatoes into 2cm (3/4 inch) cubes, and slice the onions and red bell peppers into 1cm (1/2 inch) thick rounds.
In a large bowl, toss the chopped vegetables with the olive oil, Madras paste, salt, and pepper until they are evenly coated.
Transfer the vegetables to a large roasting pan or Dutch oven, cut side up if roasting vegetables with a natural curve (like bell peppers and onions).
Roast the vegetables in the preheated oven for 30 minutes.
After 30 minutes, remove the pan from the oven and carefully deglaze it with the white wine vinegar, scraping up any browned bits with a wooden spoon.
Add the water to the pan and return it to the oven for another 20 minutes, or until the vegetables are tender and caramelized.
Remove the pan from the oven and garnish with chopped fresh dill.
Serve the molten roasted vegetables hot, spooned directly from the pan.
Let the dish rest for 10-15 minutes before serving to allow the juices to redistribute and the vegetables to retain their heat.
Stir the vegetables gently before serving to distribute the juicy sauce throughout.
